title: Idempotent recovery semantics + event-deduplication on replay
goal: Land the idempotency layer that ensures replaying the same EventLedger event multiple times produces the same final state and does not double-apply side effects. Folded WP-1-Session-Crash-Recovery-Checkpointing-v1 stub intent: 'idempotent recovery/resume for interrupted model_run jobs with deterministic failure modes.'
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/idempotency.rs with `IdempotencyKey`, `IdempotencyLedger`, `IdempotentApply` trait; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/idempotency_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-190 SessionCheckpoint and MT-192 replay primitives merged.
implementation_notes: Some EventLedger events have external side effects (e.g., Postgres writes, mailbox message posts, file system writes) that must NOT re-execute on replay. Solution: an IdempotencyLedger table keyed by (session_id, event_seq, side_effect_kind) recording 'this side effect already executed for this event'. `IdempotencyKey { session_id: Uuid v7, event_seq: i64, side_effect_kind: SideEffectKind }`. Postgres table `kernel_idempotency_ledger` with UNIQUE constraint on the key (enforces single-application at db level). `IdempotentApply` trait: `async fn try_apply(key: IdempotencyKey, op: impl FnOnce() -> Future<Result<...>>) -> Result<ApplyOutcome { Applied | AlreadyApplied | Failed { err } }>`. Algorithm: (1) BEGIN; (2) INSERT idempotency row with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; (3) if 0 rows inserted -> return AlreadyApplied (rollback); (4) execute op; (5) on op success -> COMMIT, return Applied; (6) on op failure -> ROLLBACK (idempotency row removed), return Failed. SideEffectKind enum: `MailboxMessagePost`, `PostgresWrite { table }`, `FileSystemWrite { path_hash }`, `ProcessSpawn`, `LeaseAcquisition`. Each side effect in cluster X (MT-179 mailbox post, MT-184 job state transition, MT-188 outcome record, MT-189 executor steps) MUST wrap its mutating call in `IdempotentApply::try_apply(key, ...)` where key derives from (session_id, event_seq, side_effect_kind). MT-192 replay invokes state_apply which then routes through IdempotentApply, so re-replay sees AlreadyApplied for every previously-executed side effect. Tests: (a) try_apply twice with same key — first Applied, second AlreadyApplied; (b) parallel try_apply from 8 tasks — exactly one Applied; (c) op failure rolls back idempotency row so retry can succeed; (d) replay of 10 events with 5 side effects — second replay re-applies 0 side effects.
risk_if_missed: Without idempotency, restart resume (MT-193) double-posts mailbox messages, double-acquires leases, and double-runs MT outcomes; recovery becomes worse than crash; the cluster X 'idempotent recovery' invariant is violated.
validator_focus: UNIQUE constraint at db level (not just app); parallel-apply test asserts exactly-one-Applied; rollback on op failure restores idempotency state for retry.

implementation_record.summary: MT-194 idempotency was upgraded from side-effect-kind-only dedupe to target-specific dedupe. SideEffectKind now has PostgresWriteTarget { table } and FileSystemWriteTarget { path_key } constructors, and IdempotencyLedger stores deterministic exact target keys in the existing side_effect_kind TEXT column using len+hex encoding rather than lossy hashes. The contract-named process_ledger::idempotency re-export remains, and both in-memory and Postgres-backed ledgers now prove same session/event writes to different tables or filesystem targets apply independently while duplicates of the same target dedupe. MT-193 production startup wiring now consumes these target-specific constructors for concrete Postgres side effects, so the prior dependent-runtime-verification note is closed.
implementation_record.contract_deviation_note: PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT remains: the durable implementation lives in src/backend/handshake_core/src/session_checkpoint/idempotency.rs and is re-exported from src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/idempotency.rs. The target-specific side-effect deviation is closed: Postgres and filesystem writes now carry exact target identity through deterministic TEXT storage keys without changing the existing kernel_idempotency_ledger schema.

red_team_coverage.dedupe_twice: idempotency_tests::mt194_try_apply_twice_applies_once asserts first Applied, second AlreadyApplied, and only one side-effect counter increment.
red_team_coverage.parallel_exactly_one: idempotency_tests::mt194_parallel_try_apply_allows_exactly_one_apply spawns 8 concurrent attempts for the same key and asserts exactly one Applied and seven AlreadyApplied.
red_team_coverage.failure_rollback: idempotency_tests::mt194_failed_op_rolls_back_so_retry_can_succeed asserts Failed on a transient op and Applied on the retry with the same key.
red_team_coverage.replay_side_effect_suppression: idempotency_tests::mt194_replay_reuses_ledger_and_does_not_repeat_side_effects replays ten events twice, wraps five even-seq side effects in the ledger, and asserts only five side effects execute total.
red_team_coverage.target_specificity: idempotency_tests::mt194_postgres_write_idempotency_is_specific_to_table and mt194_file_system_write_idempotency_is_specific_to_target_key assert same session/event writes to different targets apply independently and duplicate same-target writes dedupe.
red_team_coverage.deterministic_exact_storage_key: idempotency_tests::mt194_targeted_side_effect_storage_key_is_deterministic_text asserts exact len+hex storage keys, including a delimiter-like table name, so keys are deterministic without hash-collision risk.
red_team_coverage.postgres_ledger_target_specificity: idempotency_tests::mt194_postgres_ledger_distinguishes_side_effect_targets_and_dedupes_duplicates uses IdempotencyLedger::new(PgPool).try_apply against real Postgres and asserts three distinct target keys are persisted.
red_team_coverage.runtime_call_site_target_specificity: restart_resume_postgres_tests live Postgres assertions prove MT-193 concrete side-effect call sites choose target-specific PostgresWriteTarget keys for kernel_process_lifecycle, kernel_session_queue, kernel_session_checkpoint, and role_mailbox_message writes.

out_of_scope_self_evaluation.why: The implementation had to touch the folded session_checkpoint implementation and shared crash-recovery/session-checkpoint tests because the existing MT-194 primitive lives there and IdempotencyKey no longer implements Copy after exact target strings replaced lossy hashes.

next_dependent_mts_unblocked: MT-195 can use idempotency_tests plus restart_resume_postgres_tests as the cluster X.3 idempotency and failure-mode coverage input.

The earlier MT-193/MT-195 runtime-call-site verification gap is closed for current Postgres restart-resume side effects; future filesystem replay call sites should reuse FileSystemWriteTarget { path_key } when they are introduced.

Parallel apply test (8 tasks) asserts exactly one Applied outcome; others see AlreadyApplied.
Failed op test verifies idempotency row rolled back so retry can succeed.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: IdempotencyLedger uses Postgres PK + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for db-level dedupe; target-specific keys (PostgresWriteTarget { table }, FileSystemWriteTarget { path_key }) encode exact target identity deterministically; rollback-on-failure restores ledger state for retry; live Postgres test proves three distinct target keys persist independently.
